diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Right breast invasive lobular carcinoma (ILC) with positive axillary nodes
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The final diagnosis was supported by imaging studies including bilateral mammogram and ultrasound, which identified enlarged right axillary lymph nodes and the presence of bloody nipple discharge. A core biopsy of the enlarged lymph node confirmed metastatic carcinoma, likely of mammary origin, with further confirmation via second opinion review showing positivity for estrogen receptors and progesterone receptors, while HER2 was negative, indicating a primary mammary carcinoma.
